# @ic-reactor/react — AI Quick Guide

Use this file when your app installs `@ic-reactor/react` and you need fast,
accurate integration guidance.

## Install

```bash
pnpm add @ic-reactor/react @icp-sdk/core @tanstack/react-query
pnpm add @icp-sdk/auth # optional auth helpers
```

## Start Here

- Fastest setup: `defineReactor(...)`
- Existing reactor instance: `createActorHooks(reactor)`
- Reusable shared operations: `createQuery(...)`, `createMutation(...)`

## Inside React vs Outside React (critical)

- Inside React components/custom hooks: `useActorQuery`, `useActorMutation`,
  `useActorMethod`, `.useQuery()`, `.useMutation()`
- Outside React (loaders/actions/services/tests): `.fetch()`, `.prefetch()`,
  `.invalidate()`, `.getCacheData()`, `.execute(args)`

Never call React hooks in loaders, actions, route modules, scripts, or tests.

## Display vs Raw Values

- Prefer `display: true` in `defineReactor(...)` (or `DisplayReactor`) for
  UI/form code.
- Use raw `Reactor` when you intentionally need native `bigint`/`Principal`.

## Mutation Error Handling

For canister `Result.Err` variants, use `onCanisterError`. Keep `onError` for
network/agent and unexpected errors.

## Generated Files

Do not hand-edit generated `index.generated.ts` files. Put app-specific code in
`index.ts` wrappers or separate factory modules.

## Full References

- Full AI guide: https://ic-reactor.b3pay.net/llms-full.txt
- React package docs: https://ic-reactor.b3pay.net/v4/packages/react
- Skill install: `npx skills add B3Pay/ic-reactor-skills --full-depth --skill ic-reactor-hooks`
